zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Hussey於1318年出生在英國多瑟特時，她的父親Henry Hughes III27歲，母親Katherine FitzAlan12歲。她和Sir John Dalton sr至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1409年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾比斯法姆享年91歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice de Hussey于1318年出生在英国多瑟特时，她的父亲Henry Hughes III27岁，母亲Katherine FitzAlan12岁。她和Sir John Dalton sr至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1409年在英国英格兰藍卡爾比斯法姆享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hussey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for someone who habitually wore a distinctive pair of boots or gaiters, from Old French hosed, hoset, housé, Middle English hosey, hus(s)y,‘booted, gaitered’ (from Latin hosatus).

English: status name or nickname from Middle English hus(e)wyf ‘mistress of a family; wife of a householder’ (a compound of Old English hūs ‘house’ + wīf ‘woman’). Though originally a woman's name, it is often found as a male surname, presumably in a derogatory sense. The vocabulary word became hussie, with the meaning ‘disreputable woman’, in the 16th century; the surname, however, is not associated with this meaning.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa ‘descendant of Eodhus’. This was the name of a bardic family associated with the Maguires of Fermanagh, also Anglicized as Oswell or Oswald .

variants.name: Hosea
meanings: English: variant of   Hussey  . Similarity with the Old Testament prophet's name Hosea (see 3 below) is accidental.
English: in Scotland, where the name is more common, it is sometimes a variant of   Hosie  , itself a form of   Hussey  , or else a variant of   Hossack  .
African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the Biblical personal name Hosea. In the Old Testament, Hosea was a prophet who married a prostitute in order to illustrate Israel's unfaithfulness to God.

variants.name: Hosie
meanings: Scottish (northeast Scotland): variant of Irish   Hussey  .

variants.name: Oswell
meanings: Irish (Ulster): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa ‘descendant of Eodhus’ (see Hussey ).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Hussey was born in 1318, in Dorset, England, her father, Henry Hughes III, was 27 and her mother, Katherine FitzAlan, was 12. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir John Dalton sr. She died in 1409, in Bispham, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice de Hussey est née en 1318, à Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Henry Hughes III, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Katherine FitzAlan, avait 12 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir John Dalton sr. Elle est décédée en 1409, à Bispham, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice de Hussey 1318 in Dorset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry Hughes III 27 und ihre Mutter Katherine FitzAlan 12. Sie hatte mit Sir John Dalton sr mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1409 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Bispham, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.
